title: Fit a linear discriminant analysis model and write exports in one call
summary: Fits LDA on a wide-format CSV with a class-label column (1..=K) and writes coefficients, hit rates, and an xlsx report in a single command.
description: Reads a wide-format training CSV (one row per observation), separates the response column from the feature columns, and fits a Fisher linear discriminant analysis. Produces an xlsx report with fitted coefficients, training hit rates, optional holdout hit rates, and a run-info sheet. Emits a single started / finished event pair.
usage:
[["model-dfa::fit-finalize"]]
input = "<wide.csv>"
run_dir = "<dir>"
run_name = "dfa_run"
y_col = "y"
# optional:
test_input = "<holdout.csv>"
x_cols = "x1,x3,x5-x8"

arguments.name: input
arguments.description: Path to the wide-format CSV. One row per observation; one column holds the class label, the rest are numeric features.

arguments.name: run_dir
arguments.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist. Artefacts land in `{run_dir}/ocinfo/{run_name}_dfa/`.

arguments.name: run_name
arguments.description: Short identifier for this run. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

arguments.name: y_col
arguments.description: Name or 1-based index of the class-label column. Labels must be exactly 1..=K with no gaps.

arguments.name: x_cols
arguments.description: Comma-separated specifier picking which columns to use as features. Accepts names, 1-based indices, and ranges like `3-7`. Default: all columns except y_col.

arguments.name: test_input
arguments.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation. Must have the same column layout as `input`.

arguments.name: headers
arguments.description: Whether the input CSVs have a header row. Default `true`.

arguments.name: random_seed
arguments.description: Random seed. `0` means non-deterministic. (LDA fit itself is deterministic; reserved for future K-fold splits.)

arguments.name: threads
arguments.description: Reserved for API parity across `model-dfa` commands. A single LDA fit has no parallelism to exploit, so this parameter is accepted but currently ignored.

details: LDA coefficients `w_k = Σ⁻¹ μ_k` are produced via a Cholesky solve on the pooled within-class covariance. Intercepts are `-0.5 μ_k' w_k + ln(prior_k)`. Predicted class is the argmax of `β_k' [1 | x]`. The training CSV must have labels exactly `1..=K` (no gaps, no zeros). Singular designs (e.g. a constant feature column) are rejected with a LinAlg error.
value:
On success, `{run_dir}/ocinfo/{run_name}_dfa/` contains:

- `spec.json` — run configuration snapshot.
- `fit.json` — fitted LDA parameters (betas, priors, fit_cols, n_train, k, p).
- `{run_name}_dfa_coefficients.csv` — tidy coefficient table (intercept + one row per feature × K classes).
- `{run_name}_dfa_report.xlsx` — multi-sheet workbook (coefficients + training hit rates + optional holdout hit rates + run info).

The primary xlsx is also copied to `{run_dir}/{run_name}_dfa.xlsx` for discoverability.
notes: Class labels must be exactly `1..=K` (no gaps or zero-indexing).
A singular pooled covariance (e.g. one constant feature column) is rejected rather than silently regularised — remove the offending feature or add a `vcov_ridge` in a future release.

see_also.description: Cross-validated LDA fit over a folds directory (Phase B).

examples.title: Fit-finalize with an explicit feature subset

fields.name: run_dir
fields.description: Output directory for results. Created if it does not exist.

fields.name: run_name
fields.description: Short identifier for this run. Must not contain path separators or '..'.

fields.name: y_col
fields.description: Name or 1-based index of the class-label column.

fields.name: x_cols
fields.description: Comma-separated specifier picking feature columns (names, indices, or ranges).

fields.name: test_input
fields.description: Optional holdout CSV for out-of-sample evaluation.

fields.name: headers
fields.description: Whether the CSV has a header row. Default: true.

fields.name: threads
fields.description: Reserved for API parity; currently unused.
